beats.narration: Air-coupled testing throws away the couplant entirely. Nothing wet touches the part: sound crosses an air gap in, and out.

payload.title: AIR-COUPLED UT
payload.sub: Ultrasonic testing with no couplant at all.

beats.narration: Blame acoustic impedance, density times velocity. Air's tiny, a solid's enormous: each air-to-solid interface reflects nearly everything. Two crossings can lose over one hundred decibels.

payload.heading: The impedance wall: air vs. solid
payload.note: TWO AIR INTERFACES · OVER 100 DB LOST

beats.narration: Engineers fight back with three moves: drop the frequency below one megahertz, build transducers matched to air, and switch to through transmission.

payload.heading: Three moves make air coupling work

beats.narration: It shines on light materials: composites, honeycomb, foam, paper. A flaw blocks the beam, casting a shadow. No depth reading, two sides required.

payload.heading: Where air coupling earns its keep

chips.s: Only that the path is blocked

beats.narration: One question, three answers: contact bridges the gap with gel, immersion with water; air coupling engineers around the loss. Know which the job needs.

payload.sub: Know which one the job needs — and why.
